title: thelypteris microstegia subsp. hirtirachis
intro: Thelypteris microstegia subsp. hirtirachis, also known as the 'hairy-leaved maiden fern', is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the genus Thelypteris and family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thelypteris microstegia subsp. hirtirachis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically to the affected areas or consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
